findings.title: Non-owning recorder setters need an explicit lifetime contract
findings.impact: The new fluent facade exposes `ObjectKeyRef`, `ObjectKeysRef`, `RemoteClientId`, and similar setters that store `string_view` values or container pointers in recorder state, while the same recorder type is also moved through async/shared ownership paths. Future call sites can easily choose a `*Ref` setter for a temporary or shorter-lived object because the header does not document which setters borrow and which own, making the API hard to maintain safely as more access-log fields are added.
findings.fix: Document the borrow/own lifetime rule directly on the `*Ref` and `*Owned` APIs, or encode it in the API by preferring owning setters for async-capable recorders and reserving ref setters for same-scope synchronous use.
